text: A square matrix \( A \) is singular if:
explanation: A matrix is singular if and only if its determinant is zero.
name: Singular Matrix

text: If \( A \) is a \( 3 \times 3 \) matrix, then \( |kA| = \)
explanation: For an \(n \times n\) matrix: \( |kA| = k^n|A| \). Here \(n=3\), so \(|kA| = k^3|A|\).
name: Scalar Multiple Determinant

text: If \( AB = BA = I \), then \( B \) is called:
explanation: By definition, if \( AB = BA = I \), then \( B = A^{-1} \), the inverse of \(A\).
name: Matrix Inverse Definition

text: The rank of a \( 3 \times 3 \) identity matrix \( I_3 \) is:
explanation: The identity matrix is full rank. For \( I_3 \): rank \( = 3 \) since \( |I_3| = 1 \neq 0 \).
name: Rank of Identity Matrix

text: If \( A \) is a symmetric matrix, then \( A^T = \)
explanation: A matrix is symmetric if \( A^T = A \) (transpose equals itself).
name: Symmetric Matrix

text: The cofactor \( C_{11} \) of matrix \( \begin{pmatrix} 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 0 & 4 & 5 \\ 1 & 0 & 6 \end{pmatrix} \) is:
name: Cofactor Calculation

text: For a system \( AX = B \), if \( |A| \neq 0 \), the unique solution is:
explanation: Multiplying \( AX = B \) by \( A^{-1} \) on left: \( X = A^{-1}B \)
name: Cramer's Rule / Matrix Method

text: If rows of a determinant are interchanged, its value:
explanation: Interchanging any two rows (or columns) of a determinant changes its sign.
name: Row Interchange Property

text: If two rows of a determinant are identical, its value is:
explanation: If any two rows (or columns) are identical, the determinant equals zero.
name: Identical Rows Property

text: The trace of matrix \( A = \begin{pmatrix} 2 & 3 \\ 1 & 5 \end{pmatrix} \) is:
name: Trace of Matrix

explanation: Reversal law for transpose: \( (AB)^T = B^T A^T \)
name: Transpose of Product

explanation: A matrix is skew-symmetric if \( A^T = -A \), i.e., \( A + A^T = 0 \).
name: Skew-Symmetric Matrix
